content.full_text:
August 17, 2004

Richard R. Rudnansky
Meyers, Nave, Riback, Silver & Wilson
401 Mendocino Avenue, Suite 100
Santa Rosa, CA 95401

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-159

Dear Mr. Rudnansky:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1 The Fair Political Practices Commission (the Commission) may only provide formal written advice to someone whose duties are in question under the Act or to their authorized representative. (Government Code Section 83114(b).) Regulation 18329(b)(2) specifies:

“(2) Requests for formal written advice will not be acted upon unless the following requirements are met:

“(A) The name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are in question are provided. In addition, if the request is submitted by an authorized representative, it shall contain a specific statement that such authorization has been made.”

Additionally, your inquiry is hypothetical, which under regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), is a circumstance in which the Commission may decline to provide written advice.

However, we have enclosed regulation 18702.4 and several illustrative advice letters for your information. If you should have a specific question about a specific official, please contact us again for advice.

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

Enclosures:

Reg. 18702.4
Tanke Advice Letter, No. A-04-002
Reese Advice Letter, No. A-02-016

JWW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\04-159
